feed.title: SAP Community - Artificial Intelligence
feed.subtitle: Artificial Intelligence Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: How can GenAI be effectively used in SAP S/4HANA implementation and support projects?
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I would like to understand how Generative AI (GenAI) can be practically applied within the SAP ecosystem, especially in S/4HANA implementation, migration, and support projects.

Specifically, I’m interested in insights on:

  • Using GenAI for requirements analysis and documentation

  • Automating test case creation and test data preparation

  • Supporting ABAP development, code review, and optimization

  • Enhancing functional consulting (FI, MM, SD, etc.) through AI-driven analysis

  • Improving incident management and ticket resolution in AMS/support scenarios

  • Any SAP-native tools or integrations (e.g., SAP Joule, SAP BTP + AI services)

I’d appreciate real-world examples, best practices, limitations to be aware of, and recommendations on where GenAI adds the most value in SAP projects.

Thank you in advance for sharing your experiences and suggestions.

Best regards,
Juveria

entry.title: AI powered products - Alignment with SAP
entry.content: Hi, I am designing and developing AI powered products specifically for SAP. I want to align with SAP to make that these products don´t already exist, and that they can eventually be integrated into SAP. The goal is to add value to SAP´s existing product portfolio, leveraging on Artificial Intelligence. Who can I contact in SAP?

entry.title: Technical RFC: Building an "Auditable Autopilot" for Material Shortages – Where is the real Excel He
entry.content:
Hi everyone,
I’m an AI developer in Germany architecting a "Human-in-the-loop" agent to automate exception handling for discrete manufacturers (Mittelstand).
The goal is to reduce the 4+ hours of manual coordination that happens when a supplier is late or a carrier delay occurs, leading to potential line stops. We are focusing on a safe, auditable "exception autopilot" model rather than a generic "digital brain."
I have a few "reality check" questions for the consultants and experts here. I need brutal honesty so I don't build a product that fails in a real German factory environment:
  1. The 'Shadow' System: When a delay hits, does the rescheduling happen in SAP (e.g., MD04/CO06) immediately, or do planners usually run a 'Shadow Excel' for 1-2 days first to figure out the impact?
  2. The Data Gap: What’s the one piece of information that is never in SAP (e.g., real-time carrier GPS, unstructured email updates) that forces a human to manually 'bridge' the gap?
  3. The 'Green Button' Barrier: If an AI drafted the SAP update and the supplier email, what is the #1 reason a German Plant Manager would not press 'Approve'? (e.g., legal liability, lack of audit trail, trust issues).
  4. On-Premise vs. Cloud: For a mid-sized manufacturer, would the IT department demand this system runs 100% on-premise for data sovereignty, or is a highly controlled cloud connection (like SAP BTP) acceptable now in 2026?
Looking forward to the insights from the community so we can build something that actually solves the real pain points.
Thanks in advance for your expertise!

entry.title: Question: Role-based access control in a chatbot using SAP Generative AI Hub
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I’m preparing a pre-sales presentation for a real use case with SAP Generative AI Hub, and I want to address a security topic that customers typically ask about.

I have been searching for quite a long time for official documentation, concrete examples, or direct answers on this, but I have not been able to find a single clear reference that explains it conclusively.

My question is conceptual: when a chatbot consumes a model via Generative AI Hub and needs to retrieve business information (for example from SAP S/4HANA), how can we ensure that the responses respect the end user’s roles and authorization levels, and that no information is exposed beyond what the user is allowed to access?

I would especially appreciate:

  • Official SAP references (documentation, guides, or notes) that describe the recommended approach for access control and governance in these scenarios.

  • Expert recommendations on how to explain, at a high level, that end-to-end user authorizations are preserved (for example concepts such as identity/principal propagation), without going into configuration details.

Thank you for any guidance or references you can share!

PD:
I've done https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/solving-business-problems-using-sap-s-generative-ai-hub
And still, i'm not 100% sure on how roles,governance and security works.

entry.title: How AI Is Improving Clinical Communication and Compliance in Life Sciences
entry.content:
Clinical communication across life sciences has become increasingly complex—spanning clinical trials, pharmacovigilance, regulatory submissions, and post-marketing safety. Teams are expected to process growing volumes of data while maintaining accuracy, traceability, and regulatory compliance.

Artificial Intelligence is helping address these challenges by improving how clinical and safety information is captured, structured, and shared. AI-driven automation can streamline adverse event intake, normalize data from multiple sources, support compliant documentation, and improve visibility across clinical and safety workflows.

In regulated environments, AI works best when combined with strong domain expertise and governance. Rather than replacing human judgment, it enhances consistency, reduces manual effort, and supports inspection readiness throughout the drug lifecycle.

I’d love to hear from the community:

Where have you seen AI deliver the most value in clinical or safety communication?

What barriers still exist when adopting AI in regulated life sciences settings?

Looking forward to learning from your experiences.

entry.title: AI with SAP
entry.content:
Core concept is as follows. In the age of AI, the most unstructured data/information is so well organised. But the most organised data in RDBMS and/or SAP systems is still opaque to business users who do not have expertise on SAP Functional and SAP Technical Side.

PrimeAISAP is a set of projects being worked to make business users TALK to SAP using natural language for all their informational/decisional needs on day to day basis.

This started as a need to one of our auto component manufacturing customer's CEO 10 years ago wanting to talk to SAP and get all answers back in natural language. We did some work on automating a layer on top of BO/SAP and text-to-speech and speech-to-text layer. The project was not successful for various reasons, but main reason is it lacked LLM like thinking. It also lacked ability to talk on variety of knowledge sources. This is what we are working on #iitb #akashmavle @AIwithSAP @aiandsap

entry.title: Joule Studio: Why is build_default required if standard plan exists?
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I’m currently setting up Joule Studio in SAP BTP and reviewing the prerequisites.

According to the documentation, Joule Studio requires an entitlement for SAP Build Process Automation with the service plan “build_default”.

However, in my subaccount I already have the “standard” (standard-application) plan assigned, which provides full production capabilities for SAP Build Process Automation (workflows, RPA, monitoring, etc.).

From my understanding:

  • The standard plan offers full functionality for productive use

  • The build_default plan seems to provide a more limited or base-level feature set

This raises a concern, as introducing or switching to build_default could potentially limit existing capabilities.

My questions are:

  1. Is the build_default plan technically required for Joule Studio, even if the standard plan is already available?

  2. Is there a dependency or integration requirement that specifically relies on build_default?

  3. Can both plans coexist without impacting existing Build Process Automation scenarios?

  4. What is the recommended entitlement setup for using Joule Studio in a productive BTP environment?

Any clarification or best practices would be highly appreciated.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Regarding Claude Mythos
entry.content:
We are startup which focuses on disruptive thinking, Advanced MCP Agents, Agentic AI Agents and other modern AI tools to build platforms on BTP Architecture.

We are planning to go for Silver partnership. Claude Mythos, which is one of the most powerful Model which has been tested in closed group of top tech companies. Capability of this model is it can hack any strongest tech company across the globe. Anthropic has shared this with 50 top most companies.

My doubt is whether SAP is in that 50 companies or not

Reference link:
https://anthropic.com/claude-mythos-preview-risk-report

entry.title: Do you think your current ERP setup supports or limits your AI initiatives?
entry.content: I feel like ERP can either really help or quietly get in the way of AI. Curious how it’s working for you.

entry.title: At what point does ERP complexity start affecting business performance?
entry.content: At some point ERP complexity just starts slowing everything down… wondering when that usually hits for others.

entry.title: Do you think your current ERP setup supports or limits your AI initiatives?
entry.content: Does your ERP actually help when you try to do something with AI, or does it end up getting in the way?

entry.title: At what point does ERP complexity start affecting business performance?
entry.content: When does ERP complexity stop being manageable and start becoming a real problem for the business? When do you actually start feeling it in daily operations, like things getting slower or more annoying than they should be?

entry.title: Guidance Needed for SAP Learning Journey and SAP Security Career Path
entry.content:
Dear SAP Learning and Community Team,

I hope you are doing well.

I am currently studying for a Bachelor’s degree in Cybersecurity and IT at Rutgers University, and I recently received access to SAP Learning resources and certifications through my university opportunity.

I am very interested in becoming SAP certified and building a future career related to SAP, cybersecurity, enterprise systems, and emerging technologies. I am also actively learning and exploring AI, and I am very interested in understanding how AI, cybersecurity, and SAP technologies may connect in future enterprise environments.

However, I am currently a complete beginner in SAP and do not yet have prior knowledge of ERP systems, SAP modules, accounting concepts, FI/CO, MM, business processes, T-codes, or SAP terminology.

As I started exploring the SAP Learning portal, I found there are many learning journeys, modules, and specialization paths, which made it difficult for me to understand where I should begin and what sequence I should follow.

Since my academic background is in Cybersecurity and IT, I would sincerely appreciate guidance on the best SAP learning roadmap that aligns with my studies and long-term career goals. I would especially value recommendations related to SAP Security, GRC, governance, access management, enterprise security, AI-related SAP technologies, or other cybersecurity-relevant SAP areas.

I would be very grateful if your team could kindly guide me regarding:

1. Which beginner learning journeys I should start with from scratch
2. How I should learn ERP concepts, SAP basics, navigation, and business processes step by step
3. Whether I should first learn FI/CO, MM, T-codes, Internal Orders, Profit Centers, and SAP fundamentals before moving into SAP Security
4. The recommended sequence of courses and learning journeys for someone with a Cybersecurity and IT background
5. Which SAP certifications would be most relevant and valuable for my future career goals
6. Which learning journey could eventually lead me toward SAP Security or SAP GRC certification paths
7. Whether there are SAP learning paths that combine AI, enterprise systems, and cybersecurity concepts

My goal is to follow a proper structured roadmap instead of randomly selecting courses without understanding the correct direction.

I would sincerely appreciate your guidance and recommendations.

Thank you very much for your time and support.

Kind regards,
Inaya

entry.title: Is SAP Missing a Bigger Opportunity in the SME Market?
entry.content:
The ERP market is changing faster than ever.

A few years ago, businesses mainly focused on digitization. Today, the conversation has completely shifted toward AI, automation, cloud ecosystems, and intelligent operations.

Every major IT company is now positioning itself around AI in some way. From startups to enterprise giants, everyone is racing toward the next innovation cycle.

But in between all this innovation, I feel one important challenge is often overlooked — ERP awareness and accessibility for mid-sized businesses.

And honestly, this is where I think SAP has both a massive opportunity and a challenge.

There’s no doubt that SAP S/4HANA remains one of the most powerful ERP platforms in the enterprise world. It dominates large-scale enterprise operations with strong capabilities across finance, supply chain, manufacturing, analytics, and business transformation.

At the same time, the market perception of SAP is still heavily associated with:

  • Large enterprises
  • High implementation cost
  • Complex transformation projects
  • Long deployment cycles

Because of this, many SMEs and mid-market companies often move toward lighter ERP platforms such as Odoo or other flexible ERP ecosystems before even exploring SAP seriously.

Now yes — SAP has already made strong moves with:

  • SAP BTP
  • SAP Business One
  • Cloud-first innovation
  • AI integrations
  • Low-code/no-code capabilities

And SAP BTP is becoming a major innovation layer for AI, integrations, analytics, workflow automation, and clean-core architecture.

But I still personally feel there is a larger awareness gap in the SME market.

Many growing businesses still see SAP as:

“Too enterprise-focused for us.”

Meanwhile, those same companies are actively investing in:

  • Automation
  • AI tools
  • Analytics
  • Connected business systems
  • Real-time reporting

Which are actually areas where SAP can deliver tremendous value.

The interesting part is: mid-sized companies today want enterprise-grade capabilities without enterprise-level complexity.

And maybe this is where the future conversation becomes important.

Can SAP simplify adoption further for the SME ecosystem while still maintaining the power of S/4HANA and BTP?

Because the demand clearly exists.

Especially now, when businesses are looking for:

  • AI-ready ERP environments
  • Scalable cloud architecture
  • Better integrations
  • Faster decision-making
  • Real-time operational visibility

Personally, I believe SAP’s future growth opportunity is not only in large enterprises — but also in making intelligent ERP ecosystems more approachable for growing businesses.

I may be wrong here, and I’m still learning in this space myself, but I’d genuinely love to hear different perspectives from the SAP community.

What are your thoughts?

Do you think SAP should focus more aggressively on the SME and mid-market segment in the coming years?

entry.title: Are SAP BTP, SAP Build, Generative AI Hub and SAP HANA Cloud restricted to RISE with SAP customers,
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I'd like to clarify the licensing eligibility for several SAP cloud products and would appreciate input from the community (and ideally from SAP colleagues with a licensing background).

Context: We are advising a customer who currently runs SAP S/4HANA on-premise (no RISE with SAP contract, no S/4HANA Cloud Private/Public Edition). The customer is interested in adopting the following services:

  • SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP)
  • SAP Build (Apps / Process Automation / Work Zone)
  • SAP Generative AI Hub (within SAP AI Core)
  • SAP HANA Cloud

My questions:

  1. Are any of these products contractually restricted to RISE with SAP customers, or can a pure on-premise customer subscribe to them directly (e.g., via CPEA, Pay-As-You-Go, or Subscription model)?
  2. Which capabilities are only included as part of the RISE with SAP Premium Plus package, and which can be purchased standalone regardless of the ERP deployment model?
  3. Are there any technical prerequisites or limitations (e.g., required S/4HANA Cloud edition, specific connectivity setup) when consuming Generative AI Hub or HANA Cloud from an on-premise S/4HANA landscape?
  4. Does the answer differ between commercial entitlement (what you can buy) and functional integration scope (what you can actually connect to your on-premise system)?

Any pointers to official SAP documentation, product availability matrices, or licensing guides would be highly appreciated.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: AI/Claude Mythos Exploitation Questionnaire
entry.content:
SAP Technical Support/Employees,

In recent months Claude Mythos has become a significant code exploitation risk that my company is taking very seriously. So much so, we are reaching out to all vendors and asking them to fill out a Word document questionnaire asap in an effort to respond quickly with appropriate guardrails.

We do not have a confirmed S-user so I cannot submit this request myself in the Support Portal nor can the generic support assist. Can someone from SAP please reply with instructions on how I can submit the questionnaire file for review and response?

Thanks,

Brad Martz * bmartz@eqt.com * EQT Corporation

entry.title: Is it possible to query Joule via an API?
entry.content:
Hi everyone,
I'm exploring the possibility of integrating SAP Joule with an external system.
My question is: does SAP currently expose an API to send queries/prompts to Joule programmatically and receive its responses, similar to how one would call a generative AI endpoint?
Specifically, I'd like to understand:

Is there an official API (REST or otherwise) to interact with Joule outside of its native UI?
If so, what licensing or SAP BTP setup is required to access it?
Are there any current limitations on which Joule capabilities (e.g., Joule for SAP S/4HANA vs. Joule Studio/custom skills) can be accessed this way?
Is this documented anywhere, or is it still in early access/roadmap stage?

Any guidance, documentation links, or experiences from those who've tried this would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you!

Joule Artificial Intelligence

entry.title: Building an Enterprise SAP AI Operations Copilot: A Practical Approach to Intelligent SAP Support
entry.content:
Every SAP implementation eventually reaches the same stage Production Support. As organizations grow, support teams receive hundreds of requests every month. Many of these requests are repetitive and are not caused by system defects, but by business process misunderstandings, authorization issues, workflow delays, master data validations, or integration failures.
Typical examples include:

  • Unable to create a Purchase Order
  • Invoice posting failures
  • Sales Orders blocked
  • Workflow approvals pending
  • User authorization issues
  • SAP Integration Suite interface failures
  • Background job failures

Although these issues are common, users still need to create support tickets and wait for the support team to investigate.

This made me think differently.

What if every SAP user had an intelligent assistant that could understand the business process, analyze the issue, and provide the right guidance before a support ticket was ever created.

This idea led to the design of an Enterprise SAP AI Operations Copilot.

The objective was simple:

  • Reduce repetitive support tickets.
  • Provide immediate guidance to business users.
  • Improve user experience.
  • Allow SAP support teams to focus on complex business problems rather than repetitive operational issues.

Solution Overview:
The Enterprise SAP AI Operations Copilot is designed on SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP).
Instead of acting as a simple chatbot, it combines enterprise knowledge, live SAP data, business process understanding, and AI reasoning.
When a business user asks a question, the Copilot:

  • Understands the SAP process.
  • Retrieves enterprise knowledge.
  • Validates live SAP information.
  • Determines the probable root cause.
  • Suggests the appropriate resolution.
  • Automatically escalates the issue when technical intervention is required.

This creates a much faster and more intelligent support experience.

Architecture Overview:How the Solution Works

Step 1 – User Raises a Question
Instead of creating a support ticket, the business user simply asks the AI Copilot.

Example: Why can’t I create Purchase Order 4XXXXXXXXX?
The Copilot immediately begins analyzing the request. 

Step 2 – Understanding the Business Context
The first responsibility of the Copilot is to understand what the user is asking.
It identifies:

  • SAP Module
  • Business Process
  • Business Object
  • User Context

In this example, it identifies:

Module: Procurement
Business Object: Purchase Order

Step 3 – Searching Enterprise Knowledge

The Copilot searches enterprise knowledge that has been prepared by the SAP project team.
This knowledge includes:

  • Business Process Documents
  • Functional Specifications
  • Standard Operating Procedures
  • Configuration Documents
  • SAP Notes
  • Previous Incident Resolutions
  • Integration Documentation
  • Support Runbooks
  • Frequently Asked Questions

Unlike traditional chatbots, the Copilot searches only trusted enterprise knowledge.

Step 4 – Validating Live SAP Data
The next step is validating the issue against the SAP system.
Using SAP APIs, the Copilot performs live validations.

For a Purchase Order issue, it checks:

  • Vendor Status
  • Purchasing Organization
  • Material Availability
  • User Authorization
  • Approval Workflow
  • Master Data Validation
  • Related Integration Status

This allows the AI to understand the actual business situation rather than simply searching documentation.

Step 5 – AI Reasoning
After collecting information from SAP and the enterprise knowledge repository, the AI determines the root cause.For example, suppose the Vendor has been blocked.

Instead of showing a technical SAP error message, the Copilot explains:

Vendor 1XXXX is blocked for Purchasing Organization 1XXX. This is a business restriction rather than a system issue. Please submit a Vendor Activation Request using the Procurement workflow.

The user immediately understands:

  • Why the issue occurred.
  • Who owns the issue.
  • What action needs to be taken.

No support ticket is required.

 Why This Solution Is Different:

Traditional chatbots simply search documents. The Enterprise SAP AI Operations Copilot goes much further.

It combines:

  • Enterprise Knowledge
  • SAP Business Processes
  • Live SAP Data
  • AI Reasoning
  • Operational Knowledge

This enables the Copilot to provide meaningful business guidance rather than generic answers.

Business Benefits:

The solution can provide significant operational benefits.

  • Faster resolution of common SAP issues.
  • Reduced repetitive support tickets.
  • Improved first-time resolution.
  • Better employee experience.
  • Consistent troubleshooting across SAP modules.
  • Reduced investigation effort for SAP support teams.
  • Better utilization of SAP functional and technical consultants.
  • Enterprise knowledge becomes available whenever it is needed. 

Conclusion:

Artificial Intelligence should not replace SAP consultants.Instead, it should make enterprise knowledge available to every business user at the moment they need it.The Enterprise SAP AI Operations Copilot demonstrates how SAP Business Technology Platform, enterprise knowledge, and AI can work together to transform traditional support into an intelligent digital experience.

Rather than waiting for answers, users receive immediate guidance.SAP support teams can focus on innovation and business transformation.As organizations continue their SAP modernization journey, combining AI with enterprise business knowledge presents a practical opportunity to improve user experience, increase operational efficiency, and build a smarter support organization.

entry.title: SAP ACC APAC 2026
entry.content: Hi, im attending SAP ACC APAC 2026 and it was great!

entry.title: Architecting the Autonomous Enterprise Webinar Series - two sessions this week on 14th and 16th July
entry.content:
We have Two sessions coming up this week in the Architecting the Autonomous Enterprise webinar series, both directly relevant for anyone making architectural decisions around AI.
 
Architecting the Autonomous Enterprise is a 13-part webinar series designed by architects for architects. Each session unpacks a different dimension of the Autonomous Enterprise: from the Business AI Platform and agent development to data foundations, governance, and the Context System. It is a community learning journey where SAP architects, product leaders, and customers come together to work through the What, why, and How of building for an agentic world. All sessions are complimentary and available on-demand.
 
This week We have two sessions!
 
Session 7 - Monday 14 July | 17:00 CET / 11:00 ET / 08:00 PT An Architect's Guide to the SAP Family of Models With Markus Kohler, Simon Knehr , and Ashok Veilumuthu
SAP is the only company that has trained AI models on the full depth of its institutional knowledge — decades of business process expertise, application code, financial outcomes, and industry best practices. This session explores what the SAP Family of Models is, how business context delivers 3.5x higher prediction quality than generic LLMs, and what it means for architects evaluating AI model strategies. If you are deciding between generic foundation models and purpose-built enterprise models — or trying to explain the difference to your leadership — this is the session.
 
Session 8 - Wednesday 16 July | 14:00 CET / 08:00 ET / 05:00 PT -  The AI North Star Architecture and AI Golden Path: From Reference to Reality
 
Every architect asks: what does the target architecture look like, and how do I get there? This session answers both - covering the AI-Native North Star Architecture (how AI, data, applications, and agents converge as a unified system), the AI Golden Path (a guided decision framework from idea to production) and the Architecture Center in General. Includes live demonstrations and worked examples showing how reference patterns translate into real implementation decisions. If you have been looking at the published assets on the SAP Architecture Center and wondering how to apply them, this is where it comes together.
Register once, attend any session: https://events.sap.com/eaa-autent-web/en_us/home.html

You can download and import the updated calendar file to ensure all remaining sessions are added to your calendar.

 Slides and recordings from previous session here: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/slides-amp-recordings-from-the-13-part-architecting-the-autonomous/ba-p/14432238#M191674

Feel free to share this with to anyone in your architecture, AI, or technology leadership teams who would benefit.
